market_summary: Risk-on day driven by Iran peace talks optimism. S&P 500 +1.1%, Nasdaq +1.8%. Oil dropped $6 to $93 as ceasefire held and negotiations resumed. JPMorgan beat Q1 expectations (EPS $5.94, +13% YoY) but Wells Fargo missed. Tech led gains (Oracle +5%, NVDA/PLTR continuing climb). Bitcoin +5.2% to $74.4K, ETH +8.1% to $2.37K on risk-on sentiment. Gold pulled back from $4.9K range, silver stabilizing ~$75.90.
key_events_tomorrow: April 15: No major scheduled releases (PPI was today Apr 14, Retail Sales Apr 21)
April 15: Tax Day USA — potential volatility from fund flows and tax-loss selling
April 15: Continued US-Iran negotiations — ceasefire expiry approaching
April 21: Advance Monthly Retail Sales for March 2026
Next FOMC: Check schedule — potential May/June meeting window
overnight_risks: US-Iran ceasefire fragile — Trump announced Hormuz blockade on Iranian oil, but both sides still negotiating
Oil at $93-100 — any breakdown in talks could spike to $110+ quickly
Asia open: risk-on continuation likely given US session strength
Tax Day selling pressure possible — some forced liquidation
MSTR added 13,927 BTC for $1B at avg $71.9K — if BTC drops below 70K, unrealized loss pressure

instrument_news.BTC-USD: At $74.4K (+5.2%). Strategy/MSTR added 13.9K BTC for $1B at $71.9K avg. Total holdings now 766,970 BTC (~$58B). ATH was $128.2K (Oct 2025). TD Cowen target $140K by late 2026. Risk-on sentiment helping but still well below ATH.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: At $2.37K (+8.1%). Outperforming BTC this week (+12.4% vs +8.1% weekly). ETH/BTC ratio recovering but still at multi-year lows. Conservative estimates $10K, bullish $40K long-term.
instrument_news.MSTR: At ~$140 (+5%). Added 13.9K BTC. TD Cowen raised PT to $385 from $350. Sumitomo Mitsui cut holdings. Pure BTC proxy trade — moves with BTC + leverage premium. Analysts coverage initiated at Jefferies/BTIG.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Pulled back to $4,740-4,766 from recent highs. JPM forecast $5,055/oz by Q4 2026. Bullish above $4,900, key resistance at $5,060. Support at $4,700. Central bank buying continues at record pace.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Stabilizing at $75.90, above 0.5 Fib at $75.54. Bullish above $73.80, targeting $79 and $90. 6th consecutive annual supply deficit (67M oz). Industrial demand (solar/EV/AI) creating structural shortage.

metals_outlook: Gold correcting from highs but structural bull intact. JPM $5,055 Q4 target. Silver supply deficit persists for 6th year. COT data (Apr 7): Gold specs 205K long vs 49K short (4.2:1 ratio, still heavily bullish but reducing). Silver specs 33K long vs 9K short (3.5:1 ratio, stable). Key: Gold needs to hold $4,700, silver needs to hold $73.80 for continued uptrend.
oil_outlook: WTI dropped to $93 from $99 as Iran ceasefire optimism grew. Brent around $100. OPEC+ output fell 7.9M bpd in March due to Hormuz shutdown. US blockade on Iranian oil shipments adds supply risk. Floor near $90-95. Binary risk: peace = $85, escalation = $110+.
sentiment_shift: Dramatic risk-on shift. Fear fading as Iran signals willingness to negotiate 5-year nuclear moratorium. Markets recapturing pre-war levels. F&G likely improving from 21 (extreme fear) range. But ceasefire fragile — could reverse on any escalation headline.
